title: scrophularia canina var. frutescens
intro: Scrophularia canina var. frutescens, also known as Foxglove Beardtongue, is a perennial plant species within the Scrophulariaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Scrophularia canina var. frutescens are believed to provide various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried root or leaf infusion, tincture, or added to ointments and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
